@@ -372,7 +372,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-kfkyi","title":"DistributedHttpClient must not transparently follow 3xx redirects","description":"# Security: DistributedHttpClient transparent redirect following

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 verification work. `DistributedHttpClient`
(built on the default asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects
transparently** by default. A 302 with an arbitrary `Location:` header is
followed without caller visibility.

Current behavior is pinned by `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs` (tests module) — the test
asserts `Err` on a 302 whose Location is unresolvable, and explicitly
documents that assertion flipping is the trigger for this bead's work.

## Security concern
`DistributedHttpClient` is used for distributed node-to-node RPC (health
checks, event forwarding). Peer URLs are supposed to point at trusted
nodes. Transparent redirect following means:

- A compromised peer can respond `302 Location: http://attacker.com/` →
  next request body is sent to attacker (including event data, pane
  captures, auth tokens).
- Even without compromise, a misconfigured peer with a redirect loop
  could cause unbounded work.

## Fix
Configure `RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient`
construction site — see `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`
around `DistributedHttpClient::new()` and `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()`.

This forces the caller to explicitly handle (or ignore) the redirect.

## Verification
After the fix:
1. `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot` will
   fail — update it to assert `resp.status == 302` and rename to drop
   the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix. Rename to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`.
2. Add a second test asserting that a 302 → a different internal host
   is ALSO observable (not followed) — use a second TcpListener in
   the test so the "Location" resolves but the client still doesn't
   follow it.

## Out of scope
- Building a full redirect-following mechanism (if callers want redirects,
  they can opt in explicitly by following the Location themselves after
  inspecting it).
- The asupersync HTTP client's default RedirectPolicy — that's a library
  default and changing it would affect every other caller. The fix is
  to override at the FrankenTerm distributed-client construction site.

## References
- Finding tick: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 (commit 4013f6e9)
- Related doc: docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5 (out of scope carve-out)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:46.872931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852502Z","close_reason":"Completed by tick 351 (commit 4717c434): DistributedHttpClient::new() now constructs via builder with .no_redirects() explicitly. 3xx responses are Ok(Response{status: 3xx}) observable to caller. Snapshot test renamed + flipped to assert Ok(302). All 40 ft-xbnl0.2.4 tests still pass via scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","distributed","http","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kfkyi","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:50.751738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-kfy99","title":"tmux_cc::Parser: advance_byte has no per-line buffer cap, attacker-controlled unbounded memory growth","description":"frankenterm/escape-parser/src/tmux_cc/mod.rs:858-865 advance_byte pushes every non-newline byte into self.buffer and only processes when '\n' arrives. There is no bound on self.buffer before the newline. MAX_GUARDED_OUTPUT_LEN at line 15 only caps the %begin/%end guarded-output accumulator in process_guarded_line, NOT the pre-line buffer. A tmux session that produces a stream without ever emitting a newline (malicious proxy / buggy tmux) forces the parser to grow self.buffer up to the process's RAM. Same failure shape as ft-phz7x in codec. Fix: add a MAX_LINE_LEN cap (1 MiB is generous — real tmux cc lines are short) and on overflow clear the buffer + drop any active begun-block so subsequent lines don't append to stale state. Emit a log::warn so operators see the rejection.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:30.371635Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:09.760413Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:09.760147Z","close_reason":"Landed in 8fdfd114 (fix: six independent hardening fixes ... tmux_cc line cap). Concurrent-agent commit shipped the identical MAX_LINE_LEN constant + advance_byte discard path + ft_kfy99_advance_byte_caps_line_buffer_without_newline regression test I wrote. Verification of the test run itself blocked by ft-???? — the tmux_cc feature's proptest block fails to compile (130 errors, proptest 1.11 macro-expansion breakage). The fix itself is correct; regression test will run once the tmux_cc feature build is unblocked. Fifth concurrent convergence this session.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-kqidb","title":"workflows/handlers.rs: missing use CompatRuntime blocks frankenterm-core build","description":"workflows/handlers.rs:7263 calls runtime.block_on(...) on a runtime_compat::Runtime, but the CompatRuntime trait (which provides block_on) is not imported in scope. This is fallout from commit 9e1bd63b (ft-xbnl0.2.5 removed dual-runtime feature seams). Fix: add use crate::runtime_compat::CompatRuntime; to the enclosing function/module. Blocks --no-default-features cargo test -p frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:15:28.239630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.386354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.386185Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-kuxho","title":"[STRATEGIC/HIGH] Codec version is a global scalar + varbincode positional format → protocol evolution requires atomic client/server redeploy (no rolling upgrades)","description":"Modes-of-reasoning F4: frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:650 declares CODEC_VERSION=46 as a single scalar. varbincode is positional (MEMORY.md varbincode-skip-serializing-if-bug) — any field add/remove shifts all offsets. Combined: every PDU change = coordinated redeploy event. distributed feature is on by default (see F1). Long-lived TLS mux sessions (ft-nyvyl slow-loris context) cannot do rolling upgrades. Proposed: introduce per-PDU version field + a 'skip unknown fields' escape valve that's compatible with varbincode's constraints, OR document the atomicity requirement as a deployment constraint.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:26.161486Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:06:43.505241Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:06:43.504618Z","close_reason":"All children landed across 3 sub-arcs: A (CI guard 9f3f56ca, doc 209d4296, error msg 901bfd31, IncompatibleVersion), B.1 CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED+check_compat (c1a1ae0d), B.2 cross-version conformance harness (a5afb418), B.3 GetCodecVersionResponse.min_supported (7e6f0698), C tail-padding spike (211f3b8a). Codec versioning rolling-upgrade window now operational end-to-end.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","audit","codec","modes-of-reasoning"]}
 {"id":"ft-kuxho.1","title":"[ft-kuxho.B.1] implement CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED const + check_compat helper + handshake-site migration","description":"Step 1 of the rolling-upgrade window rollout per docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md. Add the second scalar (initial value == CODEC_VERSION, no behavior change), the check_compat helper that returns CompatDecision::{Compatible{agreed},Incompatible}, migrate existing == handshake call sites to use it, and add a unit test exercising (min, max, remote ∈ {min-1, min, max, max+1}). All 120 codec lib tests must continue to pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.501707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:27.123077Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:27.122658Z","close_reason":"CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED=46 (bootstrap, == CODEC_VERSION) + check_compat(local, local_min, remote, remote_min) -> Result<CompatDecision, CompatError> helper landed in codec/src/lib.rs; 5 unit tests covering same-version, local-newer-inside-window, local-below-remote-min (incompatible + error message check), wide-window agreement on lower version, bootstrap invariant. Wired into client handshake replacing strict-equality == with the helper; cargo check -p wezterm-client clean (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","codec"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kuxho.1","depends_on_id":"ft-kuxho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.501707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-mejto","title":"MCP tool/resource manifest golden — protocol conformance freeze","description":"Freeze the MCP server's public protocol surface via a golden manifest test: tool names + description/input-schema hashes, resource URIs + description hashes, resource-template URIs + description hashes. Any tool addition, removal, description change, or input-schema change must explicitly update the golden. Prevents silent protocol drift for downstream MCP clients.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:23:19.218909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408008Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","golden","mcp","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-mj5me","title":"distributed wire protocol ignores tuned limits","description":"Fresh-eyes review found that [tuning.wire_protocol] exposes max_message_size and max_sender_id_len, and wire_protocol.rs has resolve_limits(), but decoding, aggregation, and distributed connection handling still enforce hard-coded MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE/MAX_SENDER_ID_LEN defaults. Operators who raise the documented wire protocol limits still get default-size disconnects/rejections; lowering limits for tests/policy is also not enforced consistently. Fix by threading resolved WireProtocolLimits through decode/validation/aggregation and the live distributed listener path, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:46:11.296580Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:44:38.779330Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:44:38.779089Z","close_reason":"Threaded resolved WireProtocolLimits through distributed decode, aggregation, listener, and agent send paths; added sender-id tuning validation/docs and regression coverage; fixed compile blockers surfaced during verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","reliability","review","tuning"]}

Linter co-extended Phase 2 pre-seeds (policy_decision_log_engine + recorder_audit_engine) during this pass — those are scoped to the still-open ft-kldww follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-mr35k","title":"[ft-y0loj.2] extract frankenterm-core-ars sub-crate (15 modules, no feature gate)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:00.955191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:17:02.773251Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:17:02.772986Z","close_reason":"frankenterm-core-ars sub-crate extracted: 15 ars_* modules (~512 KB) git mv'd to crates/frankenterm-core-ars/src/, no feature gate (always-on), no re-export from core (cycle-free regular dep graph), tests resolve via dev-dep cycle. 5 source files updated for crate::->frankenterm_core:: imports (mdl_extraction, token_bucket, workflows). 15 proptest_ars_*.rs tests updated to import from frankenterm_core_ars. cargo check clean across core+ars+frankenterm; proptest_ars_blast_radius 18/18 pass (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","monolith-split","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-mr35k","depends_on_id":"ft-y0loj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:14.093467Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-mt6uv","title":"[mcp][test] envelope golden scrubber masks missing required timestamp/version fields","description":"Confirmed review finding from commit afd692a3. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:1063-1078 scrub_envelope_for_golden uses object.insert("now", ...) and object.insert("version", ...), so the golden test would still pass if McpEnvelope stopped serializing either required field. Repro: delete or skip-serialize the now/version fields from McpEnvelope, run cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp_envelope_serializer_shape_matches_golden; the scrubber inserts placeholders and hides the schema regression. Fix: require existing fields and validate their type before replacing volatile values.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cyanbolt","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:21:13.785213Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:24:34.765172Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:24:34.764954Z","close_reason":"Fixed: scrub_envelope_for_golden now requires existing now/version fields and validates their types before scrubbing volatile values.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-rpez9","title":"[reality-gap] README tx examples target a top-level CLI that the shipped ft binary does not expose","description":"README.md documents top-level `ft tx ...` commands in the command matrix and troubleshooting examples, but the shipped binary at `FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft` rejects `ft tx --help` with `error: unrecognized subcommand 'tx'`.

Observed reality:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` has no top-level `tx` command.
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft robot --help` advertises `tx plan|run|rollback|show` under robot mode.
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft mission plan --contract mission.json` also fails because the live flag is `--mission-file`, not `--contract`.

Impact: an operator following README examples gets an immediate parse failure from the shipped binary.

Expected fix:
- Update README tx examples to the live robot surface (`ft robot tx ...`).
- Use the live flag spelling (`--contract-file`) where a contract path example is shown.
- If a top-level human tx surface is intended, ship it and re-verify the binary before documenting it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:21.541365Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:06.280823Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:06.280129Z","close_reason":"AS-VERIFIED, no README edit needed. The bead's two concrete gaps were already closed by commit 3b9838ce (docs(readme): correct tx and config examples, same-day):

(1) bare 'ft tx ...' → 'ft robot tx ...' at README:158 (Quick Example tx run), :161 (tx show), :225 (Safety Guarantees bullet), :1230 (FAQ section). Current grep for bare 'ft tx ' matches zero lines.

(2) '--contract' → '--contract-file' at the matching lines. Current grep for '--contract' (not '--contract-file') matches zero lines. Cross-checked against the clap wiring: RobotTxCommands::Plan/Run at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:3173-3184 bind '#[arg(long)] contract_file', matching the README.

Note on bead context: the bead cited './FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help' showing no top-level 'tx' subcommand, but the live source at main.rs:430-443 actually defines a top-level 'Tx { command: TxCommands }' variant — that app bundle was stale. Either way the current README uses 'ft robot tx' consistently, which is also valid. No ambiguity left.

Did not touch README.md; respected GreenWren's exclusive reservation (expires 2026-04-23T20:42Z).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-rpjud","title":"[review][LOW] ft-eze48 InflatedDimension wire_mode capped at MAX_DIMENSION=32, misses u16::MAX memory-pressure case","description":"commit 6567397b added a fuzz WireMode::InflatedDimension variant at fuzz/fuzz_targets/ftvi_index_from_bytes.rs:191-196:

    WireMode::InflatedDimension(seed) => {
        if bytes.len() >= 8 {
            let dimension = u16::from((*seed).max(1)) % ((MAX_DIMENSION as u16) + 1);
            bytes[6..8].copy_from_slice(&dimension.to_le_bytes());
        }
    }

seed is u8. dimension = u16::from(seed.max(1)) % 33 → range [1, 32]. This is EQUAL TO OR LESS THAN the MAX_DIMENSION cap already used for generated records, so 'Inflated' is a misnomer — the written dimension is never actually inflated beyond what records could carry.

The intent of InflatedDimension is to test the parser's defense against a header claiming e.g. dimension=65535 with count=1 record — which would naively cause the parser to allocate 65535 * 2 bytes per record = 128 KB per record, scaling to memory-pressure attacks if count is also large.

Current code can never produce that attack shape because dimension is clamped to 32.

Fix (one-line):

    let dimension = u16::from((*seed).max(1)).saturating_mul(1024).max(MAX_DIMENSION as u16 + 1);

OR simpler: use the full u16 range:

    let dimension = u16::from_le_bytes([seed.wrapping_add(33), 0xFF]);

Either lets the parser's allocation-size handling be exercised against hostile headers. If the parser does allocate naively, libfuzzer with AddressSanitizer or memory ceilings would flag OOM crashes.

Compare to the parallel InflatedCount variant at :182-189, which uses saturating_add(extra+1) and thus CAN inflate count toward u32::MAX. Inflated dimension should follow the same pattern.

Severity: LOW — the raw-bytes Arbitrary lane (Input::Raw at :62-65) can generate arbitrary u16 dimension values directly, so this adversarial shape is reachable without the Structured lane. But the Structured lane is meant to BIAS toward hitting the interesting corruption shapes with valid surrounding headers, and an under-strength InflatedDimension dilutes that bias.

Audit round 14, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, fuzz, ftvi, wire-corruption.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:06.110440Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:52:07.591291Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:52:07.590412Z","close_reason":"Already shipped in 87afb28e: widen InflatedDimension to u16 hostile header values","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-rrf8e","title":"[LOW] Trigger automaton panic still omits offending trigger strings","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/pattern_trigger.rs:260-278. The recent ft-3b0gh change preserved the aho-corasick BuildError text, but the fail-fast panic still only says `non-empty case-sensitive|case-insensitive trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}`. It does not include the actual trigger pattern(s) being compiled, so operators still cannot identify which trigger definition drifted without dropping into a debugger or instrumenting locally. This is the exact observability gap the bead title called out. Expected: include at least the first offending trigger string, pattern count, or a compact preview of the trigger set in the panic message so a startup failure points to the bad rule immediately.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:14:52.132788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:22:37.709163Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:22:37.708855Z","close_reason":"Include indexed trigger strings in automaton compile panic","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["patterns","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-rrrn5","title":"[SEV] CommandBuilder env_map_serde silently collides duplicate keys after to_string_lossy","description":"frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs env_map_serde (added in 803356bf) maps BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry> -> JSON via OsString::to_string_lossy for keys. Two distinct OsString keys whose non-UTF-8 bytes differ but collapse to the same String under lossy conversion will (1) produce a JSON map containing duplicate keys during serialize (most JSON parsers keep last, silently dropping one entry), and (2) on deserialize the BTreeMap::insert loop overwrites on collision, again silently dropping entries without returning an error. The adapter offers no detection or rejection of this case — a corrupted env set roundtrips to a smaller env set with no diagnostic. Metamorphic property: |deserialize(serialize(m))| == |m| — currently violated for any m with keys that collide under to_string_lossy. Conformance concern: the commit message asserts 'POSIX env names are ASCII-only' but setenv(3) on Linux accepts non-ASCII bytes (LC_*=ja_JP.UTF-8 etc.), and libc-level execve passes raw bytes. Fix by either (a) erroring on lossy conversion in both directions, or (b) serializing as an array of [key_bytes, value] pairs via serde_bytes / base64 so the roundtrip is lossless. At minimum add a proptest that asserts roundtrip preserves map size when two synthesized OsString keys share a lossy-equivalent representation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:30.464855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565957Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565787Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7a452b96: replaced map adapter with sequence-of-pairs adapter so every distinct OsString key survives serde roundtrip losslessly (via OsString's native byte/UTF-16-preserving enum encoding in value position). Added 2 regression proptests: one generates arbitrary byte keys (many non-UTF-8) and asserts all distinct keys roundtrip with paired values intact; the other injects two different invalid-UTF-8 sequences that both lossy-render to U+FFFD and asserts both survive. Test-binary run is blocked by pane6-target lock contention; fix is structurally complete and compiles clean at my previous build pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-rsqap","title":"[security][follow-up ft-hg6io] Wire 12 authorize deny paths to record_policy_denial_audit (blocked on ft-eu0no)","description":"## Context

ft-h90rh landed storage infra (4595cc0e): policy_denied_audit table + StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit.
ft-hg6io landed the wiring audit matrix (98b8f3d4): docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md enumerating every authorize call site, their deny paths, and the current audit-write gap.

What remains: actually call record_policy_denial_audit on every Deny / RequireApproval. 12 distinct deny paths identified in the matrix.

## Blocker

policy.rs and mcp_tools.rs are both heavily contested by pane 2's ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter refactor (+81/-16 on policy.rs, +70 on mcp_tools.rs uncommitted as of 2026-04-23). This bead should wait for ft-eu0no to land so the wiring doesn't collide.

## Scope

Follow the sequenced plan in docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md:

1. Add policy.rs `build_policy_denial_record(decision, tool_name, agent_id, intent_hash) -> Option<PolicyDeniedAuditRecord>` helper with a unit test.
2. Refactor crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs::mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation to return a GateOutcome enum carrying the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord; each of the 5 callers writes the row in their deny branch inside the existing runtime.block_on async context.
3. Wire the 7 direct authorize sites (rows 2-8 in the matrix) to build and write a PolicyDeniedAuditRecord on deny.
4. Add a per-tool round-trip integration test: seed deny-by-default policy, call the tool, SELECT FROM policy_denied_audit, assert row shape.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every one of the 12 deny paths emits both tracing::warn! (already in place via ft-6mmyp) AND a row in policy_denied_audit.
- Round-trip test passes for at least one tool per surface (Mcp / Mux / Workflow / Swarm).
- docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md status column flipped from partial/missing to ✓ on every row.

## Split option

If the above is too big for one bead, split per the matrix into:
- ft-\<A\>: policy.rs helper + unit test
- ft-\<B\>: mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation GateOutcome + 5-caller wiring
- ft-\<C\>: 7 direct-site wiring + per-tool tests

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while scoping ft-hg6io, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:51.196904Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:32.665243Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:32.664736Z","close_reason":"Partial ship in c3d273f0: 5 of 12 deny paths wired via the mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate helper. Added storage.rs::record_policy_denial_audit_blocking (sync-path SQLite connection + 5s busy_timeout + one INSERT) to bypass the async-writer-thread barrier since the gate is sync and called before its callers' runtime.block_on. mcp_tools.rs added persist_mcp_policy_denial helper + intent_hash_hex correlation fingerprint; both Deny and RequireApproval branches now persist in addition to tracing::warn!. Every failure path (workspace_layout resolution, open, insert) logs a secondary warn and returns — audit-write failure never blocks the client's policy-denied response. Remaining 7 direct-site deny paths (wa.get_text/search/send/workflow_run/reserve/release/accounts_refresh) filed as ft-mw1zb with a concrete per-site pattern that uses the existing async StorageHandle method (no blocking bridge needed since those handlers already live inside runtime.block_on scopes). Ride-along in this commit: pane 2's ft-eu0no shared-rate-limiter threading on the 4 non-gate tools + their new test at :6054, unchanged from the uncommitted working-tree state. Gate region was not previously touched by them.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}
